steps.title: Separate style from identity
steps.content: The passage contrasts contemporary Indigenous abstraction with West's generation, whose abstract paintings usually followed Abstract Expressionism rather than traditionally nonrepresentational Indigenous forms. Their style alone would not mark the works as Indigenous.

steps.title: Use artist identity instead
steps.content: A wrongly says cultural identity is denied. C invents Indigenous origins for Abstract Expressionist motifs. D shifts to aesthetic merit, which the passage does not discuss. The identification depends on knowing the artist's identity rather than seeing Indigenous stylistic details. The answer is B.
